itemListElement.name: BMW X3 E83 facelift 3.0si / F25 xDrive28i

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW N52/N53 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N52 or N53 typically costs £3,000–£5,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], depending on the sub-variant (N52B30 vs N53B30) and injector compatibility. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW N52/N53 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N52/N53 family earns 3.5★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED]. The N52 is widely considered BMW's most reliable modern petrol six; the direct-injection N53 is more troublesome, primarily due to piezo injectors and NOx sensors.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common BMW N52/N53 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: For the N52, oil leaks (valve cover gasket, oil filter housing) and water-pump failure are common. For the N53, failed piezo injectors and NOx sensors are the biggest cost items. Typically from 60,000–120,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the N52 or N53 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N52 powers the 130i, 330i, 530i, X3 3.0si, X5 xDrive30i and X1 28i. The N53 replaced it for EU5 emissions in the 2007–2014 325i, 330i, 530i and early F10 530i.

mainEntity.name: How long does the BMW N52/N53 engine last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular 8,000–10,000-mile oil changes and preventative cooling-system maintenance, an N52 can exceed 200,000 miles. N53 longevity is similar, but injector and NOx-sensor replacement costs typically arrive from 80,000 miles onwards.

hero.h1: BMW N52/N53 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW N52 and N53 are BMW’s last naturally-aspirated 3.0-litre straight-sixes — smooth and characterful, but the N53’s injector system adds real cost risk. Backed by consistent UK marketplace demand [ESTIMATED].

verdictRating.confidence: 80% confidence — based on established specialist knowledge, owner forums, and parts-supply validation [ESTIMATED].

rows.score: 14 (3.5★ × 4 = 14 — N52 is robust; N53 injector faults lower the combined score)
12 (N52 repairs are straightforward; N53 direct-injection repairs (injectors/HPFP) are complex and costly)
16 (N52 parts abundant; N53 piezo injectors and NOx sensors are pricier and less widely stocked)
4 (Recon cost (£4,000) against conservative £3,000 E90/E60 host values = 133% — exceeds scrap threshold)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The N52/N53 six is a smooth, high-revving petrol gem — but N53 direct-injection faults are expensive — buy the N52 unless you can budget for injector maintenance.
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts wanting BMW’s last great NA six, especially 3.0Si/N52 cars.
verdictRating.avoidIf: Buying a high-mileage N53 without documented injector, coil, or NOx-sensor replacement history.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Exclusive to BMW — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an N52/N53 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
